00_gait_speed.label: Gait speed (m/s)
00_gait_speed.info: Gait speed is computed by dividing the displacement of the center of mass by the time it takes to move that distance. A speed larger than 1.12 m/s is considered good.

01_stride_length.label: Stride length (m)
01_stride_length.info: Stride length is computed as the distance between the calcaneus positions at the beginning and end of the gait cycle. A stride length larger than 0.45 times the subject's height is considered good.

02_step_width.label: Step width (cm)
02_step_width.info: Step width is computed as the average distance between the ankle joint centers in the mediolateral direction during 40-60% of the stance phase. A step width between 4.3 and 7.4 times the subject's height is considered good.

03_cadence.label: Cadence (steps/min)
03_cadence.info: Cadence is computed as the number of gait cycles (left and right) per minute. A cadence larger than 100 is considered good.

04_double_support_time.label: Double support (% gait cycle)
04_double_support_time.info: Double support time is computed as the duration when both feet are in contact with the ground. A double support time smaller than 35% of the gait cycle is considered good.

05_step_length_symmetry.label: Step length symmetry (%, R/L)
05_step_length_symmetry.info: Step length symmetry is computed as the ratio between the right and left step lengths. A step length symmetry between 90 and 110 is considered good.
